revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:服部住吉神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Hattori-Sumiyoshi shrine.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Toyonaka|qid=Q467479|1=Toyonaka}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.762917|N|135.478722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}}} {{Not to be confused with|Hattori Tenjin Shrine (服部天神宮)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Hattori Sumiyoshi Shrine |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Hattori-Sumiyoshi shrine.jpg|250px|]]
Haiden |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|qid=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka City|1=Toyonaka}} {{ill|Hattori (Toyonaka)|ja|服部 (豊中市)|lt=Hattori|lt_ja=服部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori (Toyonaka)|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516871}} Minamimachi 2-3-31 |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 45|latitude_sec = 46.5 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 28|longitude_sec = 43.4 |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}、{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Ikitsunaga-hime|1=Empress Jingū}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |foundation = Unknown |main_hall = |alias = |signboard = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |festival = October 15 |rituals = |map = Japan Osaka }} '''Hattori Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (はっとりすみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|qid=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka City|1=Toyonaka}} {{ill|Hattori (Toyonaka)|ja|服部 (豊中市)|lt=Hattori|lt_ja=服部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori (Toyonaka)|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516871}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. As a religious corporation, its name is '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/shigaku/shukyo/index.html {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Religious Corporations|ja|大阪府所轄の宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture Religious Corporations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (link to the religious corporation directory available). It is one of the shrines representing the Hattori, alongside {{ill|Hattori Tenjin Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Main Deities - {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}、{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Ikitsunaga-hime|1=Empress Jingū}} == History == Although the time of its foundation is not clear, a record in the shrine chronicle states that the main hall, torii, and other structures were rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Ninpei|en|Ninpei|qid=Q1193887|lt=Ninpei|1=Ninpei}} ({{ill|1152|en|1152|qid=Q19645|lt=1152|1=1152}}), suggesting that the shrine was established prior to that. It was burned down by warfare during the suppression of {{ill|Araki Murashige|en|Araki Murashige|qid=Q2859472|lt=Araki Murashige|1=Araki Murashige}} by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} in the 6th year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|1578|en|1578|qid=Q6707|lt=1578|1=1578}}), but was rebuilt in the 8th year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|qid=Q10889567|lt=Genna|1=Yuanhe}} era ({{ill|1622|en|1622|qid=Q6815|lt=1622|1=1622}}). During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, it was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} (the 22nd year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} era), it was completely burned down due to a fire. After a temporary shrine building, for its full-scale reconstruction, it received the old shrine building from {{ill|Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)|en|Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)|qid=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}}, which had been relocated from {{ill|Nakanoshima|en|Nakanoshima|qid=Q852067|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}} to within {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|qid=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}}, and was restored in {{ill|1961|en|1961|qid=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} (the 36th year of the Showa era). == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall - Relocated from {{ill|Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)|en|Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)|qid=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}} in {{ill|1961|en|1961|qid=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} (the 36th year of the Showa era). * Heiden - Relocated from Toyokuni Shrine in 1961 (the 36th year of the Showa era). * Haiden - Relocated from Toyokuni Shrine in 1961 (the 36th year of the Showa era). * Noh Stage (Kagura Hall, nationally {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579194}}) - Established in {{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (the 31st year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era), it is the oldest noh stage in Osaka Prefecture, and was registered as a national Registered Tangible Cultural Property in {{ill|1998|en|1998|qid=Q2089|lt=1998|1=1998}} (the 10th year of the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} era).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notice No. 188, dated December 25 (registration dated December 11)[https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000117789 I heard that the noh stage at Sumiyoshi Shrine in Hattori Minamimachi, Toyonaka, Osaka is unusual. Is there any detailed material about it? - Reference Cooperative Database] Accessed February 24, 2019 Initially established at {{ill|Osaka Exhibition Hall|ja|大阪博物場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Exhibition Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, currently {{ill|Osaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry|ja|大阪商工会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry|1=Osaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11440103}}, the noh stage was relocated to {{ill|Osaka Tenmangū|en|Osaka Tenmangū|qid=Q385793|lt=Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|1=Osaka Tenmangū}} in {{ill|1927|en|1927|qid=Q18110|lt=1927|1=1927}} (the 2nd year of the Showa era), and was then re-relocated to its current position during the grand reconstruction of Sumiyoshi Shrine in {{ill|1977|en|1977|qid=Q2481|lt=1977|1=1977}} (the 52nd year of the Showa era). At that time, the mirror room of the noh stage was separated and relocated to {{ill|Hattori Tenjin Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}. Several noh and kyōgen performances are held each year, including the traditional wood-fire noh in August. * Shrine Office * Sacred Gate === Subordinate Shrines === * Sumiyoshi {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q514480}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Dragon God Shrine|ja|住吉龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Dragon God Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sumiyoshi-jinja 20061015 002.jpg|Noh Stage Sumiyoshi-jinja 20061015 001.jpg|Festival Mikoshi == Cultural Properties == === National Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Noh Stage == Festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * June 30: Summer Grand Purification Ceremony * July 31: Summer Festival * October 15: Annual Festival * December 31: Year-end Purification Ceremony == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|qid=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka City|1=Toyonaka}}{{ill|Hattori (Toyonaka)|ja|服部 (豊中市)|lt=Hattori|lt_ja=服部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori (Toyonaka)|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516871}} Minamimachi 2-3-31 '''Access''' * Nearest station: After alighting at {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|qid=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} {{ill|Hattori-tenjin Station|en|Hattori-tenjin Station|qid=Q3969777|lt=Hattori Tenjin Station|1=Hattori-tenjin Station}}, about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Japan National Route 176|en|Japan National Route 176|qid=Q11421416|lt=National Route 176|1=Japan National Route 176}}: a 3-minute walk from the Hattori Station front intersection. == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hattori (Toyonaka)|ja|服部 (豊中市)|lt=Hattori|lt_ja=服部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori (Toyonaka)|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516871}} == External Links == {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Toyonaka, Osaka)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai1sibu/toyonaka-city/m04_06_toyonaka_sumiyosi.htm Sumiyoshi Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|服部住吉神社|version=99639734|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:服部住吉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11516885}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyonaka]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
